config_files.notes: All graphics, audio, and display settings are stored here as Unity PlayerPrefs DWORD/binary values. Keys use Unity hash suffixes. Edit only while the game is fully closed. Session keys (xsolla_login_*, unity.player_session*, WTUILayout, LoginServerKeyName) are written at runtime — do not modify those.

Low-level Unity renderer configuration (key=value, one per line). The developer ships this file with performance-optimised defaults already applied — do not duplicate existing lines. The only user-actionable key for most players is hdr-display-enabled. This file may be overwritten on game updates; re-apply changes after an update if needed.

Build configuration deployed by the developer. Contains the GRAPHIC section with anti-aliasing add-on toggles. Do not modify AUTH_API, USE_WEB_AUTH, STEAM_AUTH, VKPLAY, or TEST_SERVER_CLIENT — these are platform authentication values. This file may be overwritten on game updates.
Server-side gameplay configuration (XP ratios, crafting times, quest flags). No graphics or performance settings. Not a target for ReFrame optimisation.
Keybinding and controller configuration only. No graphics or performance settings. Not a target for ReFrame optimisation.

keys.effect: Overall graphics quality preset index. Confirmed label: 4=Ultra. Inferred full scale: 0=Very Low, 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Ultra.
keys.notes: Confirmed via in-game Video menu: value 4 displays as 'Ultra'. Labels for values 0–3 are inferred from Unity conventions — verify by cycling through options in-game and reading back the resulting registry value. Also update UnityGraphicsQuality_h1669003810 to the same value — the game mirrors both keys and inconsistency may cause unexpected behaviour.

keys.effect: Unity engine-level quality index. Mirrors QualityValue — both must be kept in sync. Same scale: 0=Very Low … 5=Ultra.
keys.notes: Always set to the same value as QualityValue_h4053189507. Wild Terra 2 writes both keys when the in-game quality slider is changed.
Disable in-game VSync on all goals. Use G-Sync or FreeSync via the GPU control panel instead. In-game VSync adds input latency and can cause frame time hitching when the server load spikes.

keys.effect: Frame rate cap preset index. Fully confirmed: 0=30 FPS, 1=60 FPS, 2=No limit (Unlimited).
keys.notes: All three options confirmed against the in-game Video menu and live registry values. Avoid No limit (2) on performance and balanced goals — uncapped FPS causes excessive GPU heat and power draw without gameplay benefit beyond monitor refresh rate. Quality goal may leave uncapped if the user has G-Sync or FreeSync active; otherwise cap at 60 (1) or use the GPU driver frame rate limiter to target monitor refresh rate.
Exclusive fullscreen (0) gives the GPU direct page-flip access for lowest input latency. Borderless (1) is more convenient for alt-tabbing on multi-monitor setups but adds DWM composition overhead.

keys.effect: Horizontal render resolution in pixels. Set to match native monitor width.
Vertical render resolution in pixels. Set to match native monitor height.
Use native display resolution: 0=use explicit Width/Height values, 1=match OS desktop resolution automatically.
keys.notes: Leave at 1 (native) unless the user is deliberately running at a non-native resolution to reduce GPU load, in which case set to 0 and write the target resolution into the Width and Height keys.

keys.effect: Target monitor index (0=primary monitor, 1=second monitor, etc.). Useful on multi-monitor setups.
HUD and UI scale multiplier. Larger values increase UI element size.
keys.notes: Relevant for the low_vision accessibility modifier. Exact value range unconfirmed — set via Options > Interface in-game and read back the resulting registry value.

keys.effect: Master volume level. Set in Options > Audio.
Background music volume. Setting to 0 removes music overhead with negligible CPU saving.
Sound effects volume. Set in Options > Audio.
Ambient environmental audio volume. Set in Options > Audio.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Wild Terra 2 uses its own QualityValue key in parallel with Unity's built-in UnityGraphicsQuality key. Both are written simultaneously by the in-game quality slider and must always hold the same value. Setting one without the other may cause inconsistent rendering behaviour.
engine_overrides.action: When writing QualityValue_h4053189507, always write the same value to UnityGraphicsQuality_h1669003810 in the same operation.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Wild Terra 2 does not use Unreal-style sg.* scalability CVars. The Unity-based quality system is controlled entirely by QualityValue and UnityGraphicsQuality. Individual sub-settings (shadows, textures, draw distance) are not exposed as separate registry keys — they are bundled into the quality preset.
engine_overrides.action: Do not apply sg.* or other Unreal/generic engine recommendations. Use QualityValue for quality changes and manual_only_settings entries for granular per-setting guidance.

boot_config_keys.effect: Enables Unity's multi-threaded rendering job system. Distributes draw call preparation across CPU cores.
boot_config_keys.notes: Already set to 1 by the developer. Do not add a duplicate line. This is active for all players out of the box.

boot_config_keys.effect: Enables native graphics job rendering alongside gfx-enable-gfx-jobs. Provides additional CPU thread utilisation for graphics command submission.
boot_config_keys.notes: Already set to 1 by the developer. Do not add a duplicate line.

boot_config_keys.effect: Maximum milliseconds Unity's incremental garbage collector may run per frame. Lower values reduce GC-caused frame hitches at the cost of more frequent GC passes.
boot_config_keys.notes: Already set to 3 ms by the developer — this is an optimised value (Unity default is higher). Do not change without specific GC profiling evidence.

boot_config_keys.effect: If set to 1, the Unity player waits for a native debugger to attach before starting. 0 disables the wait.
boot_config_keys.notes: Already set to 0 by the developer. No action needed.

boot_config_keys.effect: Enables HDR output pipeline: 0=SDR (standard), 1=HDR. Requires an HDR-capable display and HDR enabled in Windows Display Settings.
boot_config_keys.notes: The developer ships this as 0 (SDR). Change to 1 only if the user has an HDR monitor with HDR active in Windows. HDR output adds a small GPU compositing cost; only recommend for quality goal on HDR-capable hardware. Edit boot.config while the game is closed; the file may be overwritten on game updates.

client_json_keys.effect: Enables CTAA (Cinematic Temporal Anti-Aliasing), a premium Unity add-on. Higher quality than Unity's built-in TAA but at greater GPU cost.
client_json_keys.notes: CTAA is a paid Unity asset (Asset Store) known for high-quality temporal AA with ghosting reduction. Disabling it (false) falls back to whatever AA option is selected in the in-game Quality preset. If disabling CTAA, also set CTAA_CAMERA_UI_FIX to false. This file may be overwritten on game updates — re-apply after an update if needed.

client_json_keys.effect: Applies a camera/UI rendering fix that prevents CTAA from ghosting or distorting UI elements rendered on the camera.
client_json_keys.notes: Keep in sync with CTAA_ADDON. Set to false when CTAA_ADDON is false; set to true when CTAA_ADDON is true. Mismatching these values may cause UI rendering artefacts.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Reduce to Low or Disabled on performance hardware. This is the single largest FPS lever in the quality preset — commonly worth 20–40% FPS improvement on mid-range hardware.
manual_only_settings.reason: Shadow quality is not exposed as a separate registry key; it is bundled into the QualityValue preset. If the overall preset is too blunt, adjust shadow quality individually via the in-game menu.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: 50–60% on performance hardware. 75–80% on balanced. Max on quality hardware.
manual_only_settings.reason: As an open-world MMO, Wild Terra 2 renders many simultaneous player characters and objects. Draw distance is the dominant CPU-side cost in crowded zones and towns.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Off or FXAA on performance hardware. TAA on balanced/quality hardware.
manual_only_settings.reason: Not stored as a separate registry key. Wild Terra 2 is not a fast-paced shooter — aliasing is less disruptive than in twitch games, so Off is an acceptable performance trade.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Off on performance and balanced hardware. On for quality hardware only.
manual_only_settings.reason: Not stored as a separate registry key. Adds soft contact shadow depth at moderate GPU cost.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Disable Depth of Field and Vignette on all hardware. Bloom: Off on performance; Off or Low on balanced; Low on quality.
manual_only_settings.reason: Depth of Field reduces clarity when targeting items or reading world text — particularly counterproductive in an MMO context.

notes: Registry path uses hyphens: HKCU\Software\Juvty Worlds\Wild-Terra-2. Do not use the space-separated form 'Wild Terra 2' — that path will not be found.
LocalLow data directory also uses hyphens: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Juvty Worlds\Wild-Terra-2.

Wild Terra 2: New Lands is an MMO. Perceived lag (rubber-banding, delayed skill activation, animation stutters) is frequently server-side latency, not a graphics performance issue. Always select the server geographically closest to you before adjusting graphics settings.

Individual graphics sub-settings (shadow quality, draw distance, AA, AO, post-processing) are not exposed as separate registry keys — they are bundled into the QualityValue/UnityGraphicsQuality quality preset. Granular tuning requires the in-game Options menu.

boot.config ships with Unity gfx-jobs threading already enabled by the developer (gfx-enable-gfx-jobs=1, gfx-enable-native-gfx-jobs=1, gc-max-time-slice=3). Do not add duplicate lines. The only user-actionable key is hdr-display-enabled for HDR monitor users.

client.json ships with CTAA (Cinematic Temporal Anti-Aliasing) enabled by default. Disabling CTAA_ADDON reduces GPU cost at the expense of anti-aliasing quality. Both client.json and boot.config live in the game install directory and may be overwritten by Steam game updates — re-apply changes after an update.
Wild Terra 2 does not support DLSS, FSR, or XeSS upscaling as of this profile version. Monitor patch notes for additions.
The game uses DirectX 11. Forcing an alternative API via Unity launch arguments is not officially supported and may cause instability.

Do not modify runtime-written registry keys: xsolla_login_last_success_oauth_refresh_token (OAuth credential), WTUILayout (UI window positions), LoginServerKeyName (last connected server), unity.player_session*, EulaAccepted.

Do not modify auth/platform fields in client.json: AUTH_API, USE_WEB_AUTH, STEAM_AUTH, VKPLAY, FREE_DEMO_AUTH, TEST_SERVER_CLIENT. gameplay.json (XP ratios, crafting timers) and input.xml (keybindings) are not targets for ReFrame optimisation.
